title: #204: Before You memo()
intro: Make the keys to everything you do online easier and safer. Stop resetting passwords and filling out forms. Try Dashlane on your first device for free [here](https://www.dashlane.com/fullstackbulletin).

quote.text: The human spirit must prevail over technology
quote.author: Albert Einstein

links.title: Before You memo()
links.description:
There are many articles written about React performance optimizations. In general, if some state update is slow, you need to do a number of checks and eventually you might end up memoizing expensive computations with `useMemo()`.
In this article Dan Abramov explores some simpler alternative techniques to `useMemo()`.

links.title: The Future of the Web Is HTML-over-WebSockets
links.description: The future of web app development is taking shape, and it’s changing the way we think about server-side app architecture. In this article, Matt E. Patterson shows why a new WebSockets-driven approach where the server is pushing updates all the time might become the future of dynamic web apps.

links.title: The State of Security in the Workplace
links.description: Dashlane recently conducted a survey with Harris Poll that explored what U.S. employees think about online security and password management. Over 1,200 workers responded, and one thing became very clear: many passwords are weak and reused. sponsored

links.title: Build a react-router clone from scratch
links.description: Did you ever think *"how complicated would it be to build an alternative to the famous `react-router`... from scratch?!"* Well, this article explores exactly this thought and it is a great way to learn more about React and how `react-router` works!

links.title: Building A Discord Bot Using Discord.js
links.description: An introduction to building a Discord bot using the Discord.js module. The bot will share random jokes, assign or revoke user roles, and post tweets of a specific account to a Discord channel.

links.title: HTTPWTF
links.description: HTTP is fundamental to modern development, from frontend to backend to mobile. But like any widespread mature standard, it's got some funky and there are a lot of weird things that you might want to be aware about!

links.title: This Code Does Not Exist
links.description: An extremely interesting experiment (and a game!) This website will propose you random snippets of code. The thing is, can you guess if that snippet was written by a human or if it was generated by GPT2! Effectively a Turing test for software engineers! 😱

book.title: Test Driven Development: By Example
book.author: Kent Beck
book.description: Developers face complex programming challenges every day, yet they are not always readily prepared to determine the best solution. More often than not, such difficult projects generate a great deal of stress and bad code. To garner the strength and courage needed to surmount seemingly Herculean tasks, programmers should look to test-driven development (TDD), a proven set of techniques that encourage simple designs and test suites that inspire confidence.
